nodes.name: Mangrove Forests (Kandal)
nodes.description: Critical nursery — protects 80% of estuarine fish and prawns in Vembanad Lake

nodes.name: Ashtamudi Seagrasses
nodes.description: Feeding ground — essential for juvenile turtles and indigenous pearl spots

nodes.name: Marine Biomass (Chakara)
nodes.description: Unique Kerala Mudbanks — rich nutrient surges that drive the fishing season

nodes.name: Black Clams (Kaka)
nodes.description: Benthic filters — maintain water clarity in Kerala's backwater network

nodes.name: Pearl Spot (Karimeen)
nodes.description: State fish of Kerala — crucial for local markets and ecological balance

nodes.name: Mathi & Ayila (Sardines)
nodes.description: Primary commercial stock — sustains 2500+ traditional fishing families

nodes.name: Olive Ridley (Kerala Coast)
nodes.description: Endangered traveler — uses Kerala beaches for critical nesting every year

nodes.name: Humpback Dolphins
nodes.description: Indicator species — presence signifies health of Kochi and Kozhikode shallows

nodes.name: Malabar Prawns (Chemmeen)
nodes.description: Export powerhouse — depend on pristine estuarine water quality for growth

cascadeRules.description: Mangrove cover below 60% — immediate collapse of backwater nurseries
Loss of Chakara (Mudbanks) effects commercial sardine stock within 7 days
Native stock collapse → commercial displacement within 30 days
Sardine shortage → predator migration patterns disrupted within 45 days
